headlines.title: Iran threatens region’s infrastructure as U.S. strikes expand
headlines.summary: Rhetoric sharpened on both sides after days of renewed attacks, with Iranian state media reporting strikes around Tehran.

headlines.title: Iran releases U.S. citizen in goodwill gesture, Trump says
headlines.summary: Dena Karari had been trapped in Iran since December 2024, her attorney said. Several other Americans are believed to still be in Iranian custody.

headlines.title: Zelensky ousts popular defense minister, an architect of Ukraine’s drone program
headlines.summary: Mykhailo Fedorov's dismissal, part of a rebooting of the government announced Sunday, prompted protests in Kyiv.

headlines.title: Problematic paragraph on Strait of Hormuz places strain on U.S.-Iran deal
headlines.summary: Ambiguous language on who controls the key choke point has led to escalating conflict.

headlines.title: Wildfire smoke will worsen in the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic through Friday
headlines.summary: More than 115 million people could be exposed to unhealthy air quality. See the forecast for 20 cities.

headlines.title: Key takeaways from Donald Trump’s speech on election security
headlines.summary: The president claimed China and 'deep state' actors had deceived US voters, but critics say his claims were misleading.

headlines.title: China rebukes UK over nationalisation of British Steel
headlines.summary: The UK has appropriated its last working steelworks following fears its former Chinese owners would shut it down.

headlines.title: IEA chief warns Strait of Hormuz crisis threatens global energy security
headlines.summary: Fatih Birol says 'oil security is still a critical issue' and world should be 'worried' if situation does not improve.

headlines.title: China’s support for North Korea will ‘never change’: Top official
headlines.summary: The meeting comes after Xi visited Kim, promising closer diplomatic, law enforcement and military cooperation.

headlines.title: US carries out major strikes on civilian infrastructure in southern Iran
headlines.summary: Officials report power outages, attack on train station in Bandar, as Iran says Hormuz won't return to pre-war status.

headlines.title: An analysis of President Trump's address
headlines.summary: President Trump gave a primetime address Thursday night in which he spoke about the integrity of U.S. elections — a topic he has spread disinformation about for much of his political career.

headlines.title: White House says ICE traffic stops will continue after deadly shootings
headlines.summary: The comments came following widespread reports earlier in the week that those stops would be put on pause after two immigrants were fatally shot by ICE agents in early July.

headlines.title: Flood sirens blare in South Central Texas as rivers reach perilous heights
headlines.summary: Flood sirens blared early Thursday in Comfort, Texas, as the Guadalupe River rose more than 30 feet in three hours, the U.S. Geological Survey said. The storm could bring even more rainfall than last year's deadly Fourth of July floods.

headlines.title: In primetime speech, Trump doesn't provide evidence for illegal voting
headlines.summary: President Trump raised claims that the country's voting systems are vulnerable to being "rigged and stolen," without providing new evidence of a single fraudulent vote cast in any election.

headlines.title: Dangerous wildfire smoke continues to blanket parts of the U.S.
headlines.summary: Orange skies and heavy smoke covered large parts of the U.S. on Thursday as Canada and Minnesota grapple with wildfires. Hazardous air quality is expected to continue.

headlines.title: French parliament approves assisted dying
headlines.summary: The lower house of the French parliament, the National Assembly, has given its final approval to a bill that will create a legal right to assisted death for adults with incurable diseases, potentially bringing an end to years of debate over the issue.

headlines.title: Durian export boom boosts Vietnamese growers
headlines.summary: LAM DONG, Vietnam — Perched atop durian trees in Da Huoai Commune of Vietnam's central province of Lam Dong, harvester Ho Minh Nhat carefully cuts loose each spiky fruit, knowing that many of them will soon begin their journey to China.

headlines.title: Saving Gaza's cultural heritage, one artifact at a time
headlines.summary: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip — Armed with paintbrushes and wearing surgical gloves, volunteers inside a large tent in Gaza delicately brushed dust from a stone mosaic before placing it into storage, part of an effort to preserve cultural heritage hit by conflict.

headlines.title: Tensions spur ultra-rich to diversify across Asia
headlines.summary: Investor jitters over Gulf tensions are prompting wealthy families and founders to consider shifting capital to rival financial hubs, say bankers and advisers.

headlines.title: Dialogue remains key to Sino-US ties
headlines.summary: Chinese and American businesses operating in each other's markets remain deeply interconnected and can serve as a bridge for subnational dialogue, business leaders and policy experts said.

headlines.title: Supreme Court orders States to frame policy for early release of elderly, terminally ill prisoners
headlines.summary: Supreme Court mandates States to create a uniform policy for early release of elderly and terminally ill prisoners within three months.

headlines.title: Avoid deploying Indian sailors on vessels moving via Strait of Hormuz: DGMA to shipping companies
headlines.summary: DGMA advises shipping companies to refrain from deploying Indian sailors in the Strait of Hormuz amid heightened security concerns.

headlines.title: Govt. lists five Bills for Monsoon Session, no mention of delimitation legislation
headlines.summary: Government introduces five bills for Monsoon Session, including amendments to national honour and birth registration laws.

headlines.title: Two school students among three dead as train hits pool car at level crossing in Bengal
headlines.summary: Tragic train accident in West Bengal claims three lives, including two school students, as investigation begins at the scene.

headlines.title: After ambushes, CRPF tells forces in Manipur to use armoured vehicles, avoid sudden deployment
headlines.summary: CRPF instructs Manipur forces to use armoured vehicles and avoid sudden deployments after recent ambushes, amid escalating ethnic tensions.

headlines.title: China's Moonshot unveils world's largest open AI model, closing in on US rivals
headlines.summary: Reuters: China's Moonshot unveils world's largest open AI model, closing in on US rivals.

headlines.title: Trump's fiery China allegations may threaten superpower truce
headlines.summary: Reuters: Trump's fiery China allegations may threaten superpower truce.

headlines.title: US military says it completed latest strikes on Iran, marking 6th consecutive night of attacks
headlines.summary: Reuters: US military says it completed latest strikes on Iran, marking 6th consecutive night of attacks.

headlines.title: China's Xi promotes China's commitment to AI access in speech at Shanghai conference
headlines.summary: Reuters: China's Xi promotes China's commitment to AI access in speech at Shanghai conference.

headlines.title: Stocks stumble, oil set for weekly gain on renewed Gulf hostilities
headlines.summary: Reuters: Stocks stumble, oil set for weekly gain on renewed Gulf hostilities.

markets.label: Public Joint-Stock Company Moscow Exchange MICEX-RTS

hackerNews.title: Kimi K3: Open Frontier Intelligence
hackerNews.summary: Kimi K3 is the world's first open 3T-class model — frontier performance across coding, knowledge work, and reasoning, with native multimodality and 1M context.

hackerNews.title: How Has Roman Concrete Lasted for Millennia? 1,900-Year-Old Latrine Offers Clues
hackerNews.summary: A chemical process called carbonation, which helps seal cracks, could help explain why many ancient Roman structures are still standing today. Researchers hope that the insights will lead to better modern-day building materials

hackerNews.title: Microsoft Comic Chat is now open source
hackerNews.summary: Microsoft Comic Chat is now open source. Explore the IRC client that turned conversations into comics and helped introduce Comic Sans to the world.

hackerNews.title: The Human-in-the-Loop Is Tired
hackerNews.summary: On reward functions, dopamine, and what it actually feels like when the code starts writing itself

hackerNews.title: Decoy Font
hackerNews.summary: Decoy Font combines every letter with a decoy, making it harder for AI to read what you type. Type a message to try it out or download the TTF directly.

hackerNews.title: LM Studio Bionic: the AI agent for open models
hackerNews.summary: The AI agent made for open models, built to get things done.

hackerNews.title: GrapheneOS recommended for domestic abuse victims
hackerNews.summary: Tech abuse is increasing in domestic violence cases across Australia. Learn how configured DV Safe Phones help survivors reduce tracking, and regain control.

hackerNews.title: $100 AI Music Video: Claude Fable 5 vs. GPT-5.6 Sol
hackerNews.summary: We gave Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol the same song, a budget, web search, and local ffmpeg, then let each autonomously direct a music video.

hackerNews.title: M 3.9 Experimental Explosion – 147 Km ENE of Ponce Inlet, Florida
hackerNews.summary: The Earthquake Event Page application supports most recent browsers, view supported browsers . Or, try our Real-time Notifications, Feeds, and Web Services .

hackerNews.title: The Little Book of Reinforcement Learning
hackerNews.summary: The Little Book of Reinforcement Learning. Contribute to alxndrTL/little-book-rl development by creating an account on GitHub.

summarySections.title: News and markets
sentences: Global headlines: Iran threatens region’s infrastructure as U.S. strikes expand (Rhetoric sharpened on both sides after days of renewed attacks, with Iranian state media reporting strikes around Tehran); Iran releases U.S. citizen in goodwill gesture, Trump says (Dena Karari had been trapped in Iran since December 2024, her attorney said. Several other Americans are believed to still be in Iranian custody); and Zelensky ousts popular defense minister, an architect of Ukraine’s drone program (Mykhailo Fedorov's dismissal, part of a rebooting of the government announced Sunday, prompted protests in Kyiv).
Markets leaned lower, with 5 of 7 tracked indexes down; Nikkei 225 down 5.26% was the largest move.
Benchmarks tracked: S&P 500, NASDAQ Composite, Euronext 100 Index, and Public Joint-Stock Company Moscow Exchange MICEX-RTS plus 3 more indexes.
